heading: Abatements
label: 6404 Abatements
notes: Amendments2018—Subsec. (g)(2)(E). substituted “section 6664(d)(3)(A)” for “section 6664(d)(2)(A)”.Pub. L. 115–1412015—Subsec. (h). , substituted “Judicial review” for “Review of denial” in heading.Pub. L. 114–113, § 421(a)(1)Subsec. (h)(1). , substituted “if such action is brought—” and subpars. (f)(3). struck out par. (3). Text read as follows: “Within 180 days after the date of the enactment of this subsection, the Secretary shall prescribe such initial regulations as may be necessary to carry out this subsection.”Pub. children.text: is excessive in amount, or
is assessed after the expiration of the period of limitation properly applicable thereto, or
is erroneously or illegally assessed.

children.heading: No claim for abatement of income, estate, and gift taxes
children.label: b No claim for abatement of income, estate, and gift taxes
children.text: No claim for abatement shall be filed by a taxpayer in respect of an assessment of any tax imposed under subtitle A or B.

children.heading: Small tax balances
children.label: c Small tax balances
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to abate the unpaid portion of the assessment of any tax, or any liability in respect thereof, if the Secretary determines under uniform rules prescribed by the Secretary that the administration and collection costs involved would not warrant collection of the amount due.

children.heading: Assessments attributable to certain mathematical errors by Internal Revenue Service
children.label: d Assessments attributable to certain mathematical errors by Internal Revenue Service
children.text: In the case of an assessment of any tax imposed by chapter 1 attributable in whole or in part to a mathematical error described in section 6213(g)(2)(A), if the return was prepared by an officer or employee of the Internal Revenue Service acting in his official capacity to provide assistance to taxpayers in the preparation of income tax returns, the Secretary is authorized to abate the assessment of all or any part of any interest on such deficiency for any period ending on or before the 30th day following the date of notice and demand by the Secretary for payment of the deficiency.

children.heading: Abatement of interest attributable to unreasonable errors and delays by Internal Revenue Service
children.label: e Abatement of interest attributable to unreasonable errors and delays by Internal Revenue Service
A
children.text: any deficiency attributable in whole or in part to any unreasonable error or delay by an officer or employee of the Internal Revenue Service (acting in his official capacity) in performing a ministerial or managerial act, or

children.label: B
children.text: any payment of any tax described in section 6212(a) to the extent that any unreasonable error or delay in such payment is attributable to such an officer or employee being erroneous or dilatory in performing a ministerial or managerial act,

children.heading: Interest abated with respect to erroneous refund check
children.label: 2 Interest abated with respect to erroneous refund check
A
children.text: the taxpayer (or a related party) has in any way caused such erroneous refund, or

children.label: B
children.text: such erroneous refund exceeds $50,000.

children.heading: Abatement of any penalty or addition to tax attributable to erroneous written advice by the Internal Revenue Service
children.label: f Abatement of any penalty or addition to tax attributable to erroneous written advice by the Internal Revenue Service

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall abate any portion of any penalty or addition to tax attributable to erroneous advice furnished to the taxpayer in writing by an officer or employee of the Internal Revenue Service, acting in such officer’s or employee’s official capacity.

children.label: A
children.text: the written advice was reasonably relied upon by the taxpayer and was in response to a specific written request of the taxpayer, and

children.label: B
children.text: the portion of the penalty or addition to tax did not result from a failure by the taxpayer to provide adequate or accurate information.

children.heading: Suspension of interest and certain penalties where Secretary fails to contact taxpayer
children.label: g Suspension of interest and certain penalties where Secretary fails to contact taxpayer
i
children.text: the date on which the return is filed; or

children.label: ii
children.text: the due date of the return without regard to extensions,

children.heading: Separate application
children.label: B Separate application
children.text: This paragraph shall be applied separately with respect to each item or adjustment.

children.label: A
children.text: any penalty imposed by section 6651;

children.label: B
children.text: any interest, penalty, addition to tax, or additional amount in a case involving fraud;

children.label: C
children.text: any interest, penalty, addition to tax, or additional amount with respect to any tax liability shown on the return;

children.label: D
children.text: any interest, penalty, addition to tax, or additional amount with respect to any gross misstatement;

children.label: E
children.text: any interest, penalty, addition to tax, or additional amount with respect to any reportable transaction with respect to which the requirement of section 6664(d)(3)(A) is not met and any listed transaction (as defined in 6707A(c)); or

children.label: F
children.text: any criminal penalty.

children.label: A
children.text: beginning on the day after the close of the 36-month period under paragraph (1); and

children.label: B
children.text: ending on the date which is 21 days after the date on which notice described in paragraph (1)(A) is provided by the Secretary.

children.heading: Judicial review of request for abatement of interest
children.label: h Judicial review of request for abatement of interest
i
children.text: the date of the mailing of the Secretary’s final determination not to abate such interest, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date which is 180 days after the date of the filing with the Secretary (in such form as the Secretary may prescribe) of a claim for abatement under this section, and

children.label: B
children.text: not later than the date which is 180 days after the date described in subparagraph (A)(i).

children.heading: Date of mailing
children.label: A Date of mailing
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of section 6213 shall apply for purposes of determining the date of the mailing referred to in paragraph (1).

children.heading: Relief
children.label: B Relief
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of section 6512(b) shall apply for purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Review
children.label: C Review
children.text: An order of the Tax Court under this subsection shall be reviewable in the same manner as a decision of the Tax Court, but only with respect to the matters determined in such order.

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: i Cross reference
children.text: For authority to suspend running of interest, etc. by reason of Presidentially declared disaster or terroristic or military action, see section 7508A.
